discrepancies.description: Slot 156 verdict Q5b item 3 anticipated that BT-adjacent literature might surface organically via P-009 active variant v1 dispatch; the actual E result demonstrates this expectation was not realised. The literature-cost-variance estimate for Pathway B should be revised toward the upper bound (weeks-to-months) rather than the slot-156-modelled 25-40% accessibility scenario.
discrepancies.scope: Slot 156 verdict Pathway B cost estimate
discrepancies.action: Surface to operator for slot 156 verdict amendment consideration; the Pathway B prior probability does not need to be lowered (the difficulty was already weeks-to-months), but the literature-availability conditional should be marked as RESOLVED-NEGATIVE.

discrepancies.description: Slot 156 verdict Q3d / Q5a 'recommendation: pursue E (cheap, decision-informing) and A (direct, decision-informing) regardless. Defer the B/D decision until E returns. This is a graduated commitment...' — the recommended graduated commitment has now been exercised with E completing first. The result is NULL on STRONG hits but produces a clear downstream sequence (A next, then V0+ closure, then D154-defensive stub). The graduated-commitment design worked.

Anomaly 1 in slot 156 verdict ('U2 quadrant has remained untested across multiple closure cascades') becomes more salient post-E-NULL: with no literature foothold for Pathway B/D, Pathway A (U2 quadrant survey) is the only remaining cheap structural-information-extracting move. The S154 Q5a caveat template should be updated post-A-fire to reference all-4-quadrants-surveyed framing per slot 156 Amendment 4.
discrepancies.action: Operator decision (Pathway A pre-fire substrate prep).

discrepancies.description: P-009 caveat active variant v1 dispatch is now COMPLETED at the literature-reconnaissance level. The v1 status NOT_YET_DISPATCHED at bridge SHA 1873538 is consequently superseded by this E-fire result. The next P-009 status transition is: v1 LITERATURE_RECONNAISSANCE_DISPATCHED -> result NULL. The four-tense-variant framework at p009_m8b_caveat_all_variants.md does not have a specific 'dispatch-but-null-result' verb-form variant; this represents a gap in the variant taxonomy. The conventional v1 'will supply' language is technically still operationally correct (the d>=3 binding-window dispatch as a full SIARC-internal computation has not occurred; only its literature-reconnaissance prefix has).
discrepancies.action: Surface to operator: should the P-009 v1 status be updated to LITERATURE_RECONNAISSANCE_DISPATCHED_NULL, or does the v1 NOT_YET_DISPATCHED state persist because the d>=3 binding-window dispatch itself (the structural M8b discrimination) has not been fired? Recommendation: keep v1 'will supply' wording, append SIARC-internal annotation 'literature-reconnaissance Pathway E fired 2026-05-11 with NULL result' to the P-009 caveat audit-trail.
